questions.question: Briefly explain the difference between IPSec's Transport mode and Tunnel mode. (3 marks)
expectedPoints: Transport mode encrypts only the payload of the original IP packet.
The original IP header remains unchanged and is used for routing.
Tunnel mode encrypts the entire original IP packet and adds a new IP header for routing.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the OpenVPN and WireGuard protocols. Discuss their design approach, typical performance, and key strengths. (10 marks)
expectedPoints: OpenVPN is highly configurable and uses the SSL/TLS library, operating at a higher OSI layer (4/7).
Its strength lies in stability and ability to bypass firewalls (e.g., using TCP 443).
WireGuard is a modern protocol with a minimal, auditable codebase operating at Layer 3.
It uses a fixed set of modern cryptographic primitives (e.g., ChaCha20, Curve25519).
WireGuard generally offers superior performance and lower latency due to its efficiency.
OpenVPN is often praised for its versatility and maturity across diverse environments.
WireGuard excels in use cases requiring seamless roaming (e.g., mobile devices) and simple configuration.
A key difference is the complexity of configuration: OpenVPN has many options, WireGuard has very few.
